description: Curated Gene Fitness SNP panel for GenomeInsight (P3-45). Covers 4 fitness pathways (Endurance, Power, Recovery & Injury, Training Response) with 8 curated SNPs plus GWAS Catalog fitness EFO term associations. ACTN3 R577X uses three-state calling (RR/RX/XX). ACE rs4341 is an I/D proxy with coverage caveat.

pathways.name: Endurance
pathways.description: Variants influencing aerobic capacity, mitochondrial biogenesis, and sustained endurance performance.

CC.effect_summary: RR genotype — functional alpha-actinin-3 in fast-twitch muscle fibers. Associated with power and sprint performance.

CT.effect_summary: RX genotype — one functional copy of alpha-actinin-3. Mixed muscle fiber profile suited to both endurance and power activities.

TC.effect_summary: RX genotype — one functional copy of alpha-actinin-3. Mixed muscle fiber profile suited to both endurance and power activities.

TT.effect_summary: XX genotype — complete absence of alpha-actinin-3 in fast-twitch fibers. Shift toward slow-twitch muscle physiology. Associated with enhanced endurance capacity.

snps.recommendation_text: ACTN3 R577X is one of the most replicated gene-sport performance associations. XX individuals may benefit from endurance-focused training; RR individuals may respond better to power/sprint training.

GG.effect_summary: Normal PPARGC1A (PGC-1α) activity. Standard mitochondrial biogenesis and aerobic capacity.

GA.effect_summary: One copy of Ser482 variant. Mildly reduced PGC-1α activity. May have modestly lower baseline VO2max.

AG.effect_summary: One copy of Ser482 variant. Mildly reduced PGC-1α activity. May have modestly lower baseline VO2max.

AA.effect_summary: Homozygous Ser482. Reduced PGC-1α activity. Associated with lower baseline aerobic capacity and VO2max, though endurance training can still improve fitness.

snps.recommendation_text: PPARGC1A Gly482Ser has been associated with VO2max variation. Regular aerobic exercise can improve endurance capacity regardless of genotype.

CC.effect_summary: Normal AMP deaminase 1 activity. Standard purine nucleotide cycle in exercising muscle.

CT.effect_summary: Carrier of AMPD1 deficiency allele. Mildly reduced AMP deaminase activity. Some individuals report earlier muscle fatigue during intense exercise.

TC.effect_summary: Carrier of AMPD1 deficiency allele. Mildly reduced AMP deaminase activity. Some individuals report earlier muscle fatigue during intense exercise.

TT.effect_summary: Homozygous AMPD1 deficiency. Absent AMP deaminase in skeletal muscle. May experience exercise-induced myalgia and premature fatigue.

snps.recommendation_text: AMPD1 deficiency is common (~2% homozygous in Europeans). Symptoms vary widely — many homozygous individuals are asymptomatic. Adjust exercise intensity if exercise-induced cramping occurs.

pathways.name: Power
pathways.description: Variants influencing explosive strength, fast-twitch muscle fiber composition, and sprint performance.

AA.effect_summary: Proxy for ACE II genotype (insertion/insertion). Associated with lower ACE activity and enhanced endurance performance.

AG.effect_summary: Proxy for ACE ID genotype (insertion/deletion). Intermediate ACE activity. Mixed endurance/power profile.

GA.effect_summary: Proxy for ACE ID genotype (insertion/deletion). Intermediate ACE activity. Mixed endurance/power profile.

GG.effect_summary: Proxy for ACE DD genotype (deletion/deletion). Associated with higher ACE activity and enhanced power/sprint performance.

snps.coverage_note: rs4341 is a proxy SNP for the ACE insertion/deletion (I/D) polymorphism, which is a 287 bp Alu repeat not directly genotyped on SNP arrays. Proxy accuracy depends on linkage disequilibrium in the individual's ancestry population.
snps.recommendation_text: The ACE I/D polymorphism is one of the most studied gene-exercise associations. D allele carriers may respond better to strength/power training, while I allele carriers may have an endurance advantage.

TT.effect_summary: Normal MCT1 lactate transporter activity. Standard lactate clearance during high-intensity exercise.

TA.effect_summary: One copy of Glu490 variant. Mildly reduced MCT1 lactate transport. May experience slightly slower lactate clearance.

AT.effect_summary: One copy of Glu490 variant. Mildly reduced MCT1 lactate transport. May experience slightly slower lactate clearance.

AA.effect_summary: Homozygous Glu490. Reduced MCT1 lactate transporter activity. May experience slower lactate clearance during repeated high-intensity bouts.

snps.recommendation_text: MCT1 facilitates lactate transport across cell membranes. Individuals with reduced activity may benefit from longer recovery intervals between high-intensity sets.

pathways.name: Recovery & Injury
pathways.description: Variants influencing connective tissue integrity, injury susceptibility, and recovery from exercise.

CC.effect_summary: Normal type V collagen expression. Standard connective tissue composition.

CT.effect_summary: One copy of the T allele. Modestly altered type V collagen expression. Mildly increased range of motion and potentially increased soft tissue injury susceptibility.

TC.effect_summary: One copy of the T allele. Modestly altered type V collagen expression. Mildly increased range of motion and potentially increased soft tissue injury susceptibility.

TT.effect_summary: Homozygous T allele. Altered type V collagen expression associated with increased range of motion and higher susceptibility to Achilles tendon and other soft tissue injuries.

snps.recommendation_text: COL5A1 variants have been associated with tendon and ligament injury risk. Individuals with the TT genotype may benefit from gradual training volume increases and targeted tendon-strengthening exercises.

GG.effect_summary: Normal type I collagen Sp1 binding. Standard bone mineral density and connective tissue.

GT.effect_summary: Heterozygous Sp1 binding site variant. Mildly increased type I collagen alpha-1 expression ratio. Subtle effect on bone density.

TG.effect_summary: Heterozygous Sp1 binding site variant. Mildly increased type I collagen alpha-1 expression ratio. Subtle effect on bone density.

TT.effect_summary: Homozygous Sp1 binding site variant. Altered type I collagen alpha-1/alpha-2 ratio. Associated with reduced bone mineral density and increased stress fracture risk in athletes.

snps.recommendation_text: COL1A1 Sp1 variant has been associated with osteoporosis and stress fracture risk. Weight-bearing exercise and adequate calcium/vitamin D intake support bone health regardless of genotype.

pathways.name: Training Response
pathways.description: Variants influencing body composition response to exercise, metabolic adaptation, and training-induced fitness gains.

TT.effect_summary: Standard FTO variant. Lower genetic predisposition toward higher BMI.

TA.effect_summary: One copy of risk allele. Modestly higher average BMI (~0.4 kg/m²). Regular physical activity attenuates the effect.

AT.effect_summary: One copy of risk allele. Modestly higher average BMI (~0.4 kg/m²). Regular physical activity attenuates the effect.

AA.effect_summary: Homozygous risk allele. Higher average BMI (~0.7 kg/m²). Physical activity significantly attenuates the genetic effect on body weight.

snps.recommendation_text: FTO is the most replicated obesity-associated gene. Importantly, physical activity significantly reduces the effect of FTO risk alleles on BMI. Regular exercise is beneficial regardless of genotype.

ACTN3_power_context.note: ACTN3 rs1815739 appears under Endurance (XX genotype → endurance shift), but is equally relevant to Power (RR genotype → power advantage). The three-state calling model (RR/RX/XX) provides context for both pathways.

ACE_endurance_context.note: ACE rs4341 (I/D proxy) appears under Power (DD → power), but II genotype is associated with endurance advantage. Findings display both perspectives.

ACTN3_R577X.description: Three-state calling model for ACTN3 R577X. Maps genotype to functional status of alpha-actinin-3 protein.

RR.label: RR — Power
RR.description: Full alpha-actinin-3 expression. Fast-twitch muscle fiber composition favors power/sprint activities.

RX.label: RX — Mixed
RX.description: One functional copy. Mixed muscle fiber profile. No strong bias toward endurance or power.

XX.label: XX — Endurance
XX.description: No alpha-actinin-3 protein. Slow-twitch muscle fiber shift. Associated with elite endurance athlete prevalence.

ACE_ID_proxy.description: ACE insertion/deletion polymorphism is a 287 bp Alu repeat not directly captured by SNP arrays. rs4341 is used as a proxy SNP in linkage disequilibrium with the I/D variant.
ACE_ID_proxy.proxy_target: ACE I/D (287 bp Alu insertion/deletion in intron 16)
ACE_ID_proxy.proxy_accuracy_note: Linkage disequilibrium between rs4341 and ACE I/D varies by ancestry. Proxy accuracy is highest in European populations (r² ~0.8) and may be lower in other populations. Results should be interpreted with this caveat.
